chapter_title.ar: باب الْمَسْأَلَةِ فِي الْقَبْرِ وَ مَنْ يُسْأَلُ وَ مَنْ لَا يُسْأَلُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 88 – The questioning in the grave, and the one who would be questioned and the one who would not be questioned

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (14/214)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (14/214)
text: رِوَايَةٍ أُخْرَى سُئِلَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنِ الْمَصْلُوبِ يُصِيبُهُ عَذَابُ الْقَبْرِ فَقَالَ إِنَّ رَبَّ الْأَرْضِ هُوَ رَبُّ الْهَوَاءِ فَيُوحِي اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ إِلَى الْهَوَاءِ فَيَضْغَطُهُ ضَغْطَةً أَشَدَّ مِنْ ضَغْطَةِ الْقَبْرِ .

en.hubeali: ‘Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> was asked about the crucified one, whether he would be hit by the Punishment of the grave. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘The Lord<sup>azwj</sup> of the ground, He<sup>azwj</sup> is the Lord<sup>azwj</sup> of the air, so Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic would be Revealing unto the air, so it would squeeze him with a squeezing more intense than the squeezing of the grave’.
en.sarwar: 17. The following is narrated in another Hadith: “Someone asked abu ’Abd Allah, ‘Does a person who dies because of being hanged to death, suffer the severe pressure of the grave?’ The Imam said, ‘The Lord of the earth is also the Lord of the air. Allah, the Most Majestic, the Most Glorious, inspires the air and it applies a pressure on the deceased which is greater than the pressure in the grave.’”
